judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with equivalent evidence; they are comparable, with Report 2 marginally more precise on line-number citations and Report 1 adding a bit more breadth (missing frontend, image-quality concerns).

report_1.evidence:
**"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — False. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` (Action 9) contains only a stub endpoint that returns `[]`, with a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. There is no PDF-generation code anywhere in the repository. This feature does not exist yet and the claim must be removed.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False, and directly contradicted by the code. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` (Action 10) reads: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Sending a false security-posture claim to healthcare clinics is a real risk — this must be corrected or removed.

report_2.evidence:
`app/routers/visits.py:9-11` shows the `/visits` route returns an empty list and ends with `# TODO: pdf export` — there is no PDF export implementation at all, just a stub and a TODO comment. This is the single clearest false claim in the doc and must be removed.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** (`highlights-v4.md:30`) — False, and contradicted directly by code. `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic. \n TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. 2FA exists but is opt-in, not on-by-default. ... must be corrected or removed.

report_1.evidence:
`after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen.

This is confirmed by `captures.json` (Action 5), which records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`.

report_2.evidence:
`after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES".

These are not the same surface. `captures.json` confirms this isn't a rendering fluke: `before_scheduling.png` is tagged `"route": "/schedule"` while `after_scheduling.png` is tagged `"route": "/billing/invoices"`.

report_1.evidence: `after_export.png` **does not exist** — confirmed absent from the `ls -la` listing. ... The "Export your visit history" section will render with a broken image link. ... There is one **orphaned image not referenced anywhere in the document**: `unused_capture_07.png`, which renders "PATIENT LIST" (Action 7).

report_2.evidence: **Broken reference:** the doc embeds `![after](img/after_export.png)` at `highlights-v4.md:26`, but `docs/release/img/` contains no `after_export.png` ... **Orphan file:** `docs/release/img/unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk (I viewed it — it renders "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced nowhere in `highlights-v4.md`

report_1.evidence: **Line 33: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal process note — it labels the document as a "draft" and discloses how it was produced. It should never go out verbatim in a customer email; it needs to be stripped before send regardless of the other fixes.

report_2.evidence: **The internal process footer**: `---\nHighlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` (`highlights-v4.md:32-33`). ... this line is an internal drafting note ... That's process metadata for internal reviewers, not something a clinic customer needs or should see in a finished announcement. It should be stripped before sending
